text: The surgeon establishes pneumoperitoneum and scores the peritoneum under the proximal inferior mesenteric vein, entering the bloodless embryonic fusion plane to separate the mesocolon from the retroperitoneum. The dissection proceeds laterally over Dorota's fascia to the abdominal sidewall. The inferior mesenteric vein is skeletonized, clipped, and divided at its origin. The lesser sac is entered adjacent to the ligament of Treitz, and tissues along the inferior border of the pancreas are divided in a medial-lateral fashion, allowing the pancreas to be dropped.

A retractor is placed underneath the mesocolon towards the splenic flexure. The surgeon focuses dissection inferiorly towards the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA), scoring the peritoneum beneath it. The plane between the mesocolon and retroperitoneum is developed in a medial to lateral fashion, with care taken to identify and protect the ureter. The peritoneum is lifted anteriorly to reveal remaining attached tissues, and scored again to connect the dissection of the inferior mesenteric vein (IMV) to the IMA, ensuring the ureter is identified and preserved.

The surgeon skeletonizes and divides the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) between clips using bipolar energy, ensuring tension is released on the mesocolon to prevent bleeding. The IMA stump is checked for bleeding. With the medial dissection complete, the lateral peritoneal attachments to the abdominal sidewall along the white line of Toldt are divided using monopolar and bipolar energy. The previously placed ratec at the splenic flexure is visualized, confirming the correct dissection plane.

The surgeon opens the lesser sac along and below the gastroepiploic arcade, dividing the omentum towards the splenic flexure. The colon is released up to the middle colic vessels, and the remaining attachments of the splenic flexure are divided. The total mesorectal excision (TME) dissection continues into the holy plane at the upper rectum, avoiding the hypogastric nerves, and is performed down to the pelvic floor without incident.
